expectations.text: Every input assumption has a Named Range with Inp_ prefix (e.g., Inp_Customers_Y1, Inp_Price_Monthly, Inp_Customer_Growth, Inp_Price_Increase, Inp_Churn_Monthly)
expectations.evidence: Layer 1 table lists: Inp_Customers_Y1, Inp_Price_Monthly_Y1, Inp_Customer_Growth_Rate, Inp_Price_Increase_Rate, Inp_Churn_Rate_Monthly — all with Inp_ prefix.

expectations.text: All calculation formulas reference Named Ranges only — zero cell-coordinate references like A1, B2, $C$3
expectations.evidence: All 75 calculation formulas in Layer 2 reference Named Ranges exclusively. The IDFA compliance checklist states 'Zero cell-address references (A1, B8, $C$10)' and confirms PASS for Guardrail 1.

expectations.text: The model separates into three layers: Assumptions (inputs only), Calculations (logic only), Output (presentation only)
expectations.evidence: Architecture Overview explicitly defines 'Layer 1 (Assumptions): All user-modifiable inputs', 'Layer 2 (Calculations): All formulas referencing Named Ranges only', 'Layer 3 (Output): Display tables and charts reading from Layer 2 only'.

expectations.text: Revenue calculations correctly account for churn reducing the customer base
expectations.evidence: Net revenue uses average customers (midpoint of BoY and EoY after churn): 'Revenue_Net_Y1 = Customers_Avg_Y1 * Price_Annual_Y1'. Churn compounds monthly (21.53% annual from 2% monthly). Churned revenue is tracked separately.

expectations.text: The specification includes both MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue) and ARR (Annual Recurring Revenue) as Named Ranges
expectations.evidence: Section M lists MRR_BoY_Y1 through MRR_BoY_Y5 and ARR_BoY_Y1 through ARR_BoY_Y5, with formulas: 'MRR_BoY_Y1 = Customers_BoY_Y1 * Price_Monthly_Y1' and 'ARR_BoY_Y1 = MRR_BoY_Y1 * 12'.

expectations.text: Each formula is readable as a plain-English business rule without needing to trace cell references
expectations.evidence: Every formula table includes a 'Business Rule' column with plain English, e.g., 'Year 2 price is Year 1 price plus the annual increase', 'Customers lost in Year 1 is BoY count times the annual churn rate'.

expectations.text: Year-over-year naming convention uses _Y1, _Y2 etc. dimension suffixes consistently
expectations.evidence: All yearly Named Ranges use _Y1 through _Y5 suffixes consistently: Revenue_Net_Y1, Customers_BoY_Y1, Price_Monthly_Y1, MRR_BoY_Y1, etc. The Named Range Registry confirms this pattern across all 80 ranges.
